steps.title: Focus on Mrs. Higgins
steps.content: Mrs. Higgins says Henry offends her friends and that they stop coming whenever they meet him. Her final line, “you mustn’t stay,” makes her purpose explicit.

steps.title: Match the main purpose
steps.content: Choice A is about what she does when she goes out, which the text never describes. Choice C invents a home description. Choice D invents an explanation for Henry’s visits. The exchange mainly shows that Mrs. Higgins wants Henry to leave before her friends arrive. The answer is B.
